title: The Land of Milk and Honey: What the Torah Means
title_zh: 流淌着奶与蜜的土地:妥拉的真正含义
title_he: ארץ זבת חלב ודבש: מה התורה מתכוונת
author: Torah Light Team
description: An exploration of the Torah's famous phrase 'land flowing with milk and honey' - its literal meaning, symbolic layers, and spiritual significance. Includes parallels to Chinese concepts of agricultural abundance and ideal society.
targetAudience: Chinese speakers learning about Judaism, those interested in Israel, converts and students

sources.reference: Book of Rites (禮記) - Confucian Classic on ideal society

culturalParallels.description: Ancient Chinese vision of ideal peaceful and prosperous society, similar to Torah's vision of Israel

culturalParallels.concept: 魚米之鄉 (yú mǐ zhī xiāng) - Land of fish and rice
culturalParallels.description: Chinese phrase for fertile regions where basic needs are easily met, parallel to 'milk and honey'

culturalParallels.concept: Connection between place and cultural flourishing
culturalParallels.description: Both traditions link fertile river valleys (Yellow River/Yangtze vs. Land of Israel) with prosperity and spiritual development
